creator: sko@rostwald.de
text:
(In reply to Vladimir Druzenko from comment #31)

>List of not updated modules:
>angie-module-auth-spnego
already updated (1.1.1 -> 1.1.2)
>angie-module-brotli
no new version available (1.0.0rc)
>angie-module-cache-purge
no new version available (2.5.3)
>angie-module-dav-ext
no new version available (3.0.0)
>angie-module-echo
no new version available (0.63)
>angie-module-enhanced-memcached
no new version available (0.3)
>angie-module-eval
no new version available (2016.06.10)
>angie-module-geoip2
no new version available (3.4)
>angie-module-image-filter
builds fine against current graphics/gd
>angie-module-perl
builds fine against the current default version of lang/perl (5.36)
>angie-module-postgres
no new version available (1.0rc7)
>angie-module-redis2
no new version available (0.15)
>angie-module-rtmp
no new version available (1.2.2)
>angie-module-subs
already at latest commit e12e965ac1837ca709709f9a26f572a54d83430e
>angie-module-testcookie
already at latest commit 64137c2f7e66e8866f56fdecaaf09ac10a034da9
>angie-module-upload
no new version available (2.3.0)
>angie-module-xslt
builds fine against current textproc/libxslt from ports; upstream has a new version (port: 1.1.37, upstream: 1.1.42) for which there already is an ongoing PR (bug #279741).


I also ran a poudriere bulk build of all www/angie-module* ports on my 13.4-RELEASE buildhost and all ports were built without errors. I only had to set 'DEFAULT_VERSIONS+=ssl=base' for module-auth-spnego because it needs GSSAPI from base and I'm usually using OpenSSL from ports...


I've been running Angie 1.7.0 for quite a while pretty much everywhere (i.e. at least 10 servers/jails, replaced all nginx installations with angie) and 1.8.1 for little over a week on 3 jails; no issues so far, but we're also not using modules anywhere.
